decisions.title: Why a Dispatch Map Pattern
decisions.description: Tools are registered in a map (dictionary) keyed by tool name. When the LLM requests a function call, the host looks up the name in the map and invokes the corresponding handler. This pattern is simple, extensible, and avoids long if-else chains. Adding a new tool means adding one entry to the map — nothing else changes.
decisions.alternatives: An if-else chain works for two tools but becomes unmaintainable at ten. A class-based plugin system adds abstraction overhead that is not justified at this stage. The dispatch map sits in the sweet spot: minimal code, maximum extensibility.

zh.title: 为什么使用分发映射模式
zh.description: 工具通过名称注册在一个映射表（字典）中。当 LLM 请求函数调用时，宿主按名称查表并调用对应的处理函数。这种模式简洁、可扩展，避免了冗长的 if-else 链。添加新工具只需在映射表中增加一个条目，其他代码无需改动。

decisions.title: Why the Loop Does Not Change When Adding Tools
decisions.description: The core while loop from Lesson 0 remains structurally identical. The only addition is a check: if the LLM response contains a tool call, execute it and feed the result back. This demonstrates a crucial design principle — the agent loop is a stable skeleton, and capabilities are plugged in without modifying the skeleton itself.
decisions.alternatives: Some frameworks rebuild the control flow for each new capability. We deliberately keep the loop unchanged to reinforce that agent design is about composition, not rewriting.

zh.title: 为什么添加工具时循环不需要改变
zh.description: 从第 0 课开始的核心 while 循环在结构上保持不变。唯一的增加是一个判断：如果 LLM 的回复包含工具调用，就执行并将结果回传。这体现了一个关键设计原则——Agent 循环是一个稳定的骨架，各种能力是插入其中的，而不是重写骨架本身。
